item.title: C'mon Feel the Noise! A Metalhead Dump
item.description:
Front Page Edit: thanks for all the accolades and upvotes! I'm doing my best to reply to everyone but I'm loving some of the new music recommendations. Bloodywood is high up on my priorities now
Wayne Static (RIP) is another guilty offender. The first minute or so of his song 'Static Killer' is just pornographic moaning
Metal is a huge genre, don't be a gatekeeping douchenoozle just because someone likes Nightwish which is symphonic. Or if someone still enjoys Nu Metal (guilty as charged)
These guys are pretty awesome. Definitely check out their new album Opal in Sky: Dream Shift
Luna Tax
item.dc:creator: DuncanHillsCoffee4Life

17734/36500 https://gofund.me/8b5802c4 Proud AF of his wife lol
She seems fun
When does a family become a clan?
She's a keeper
It took me a couple watches to catch it, honestly
I want to love on an elephant
Damn monkeys
He regretted once they found him lol
I miss my best friend. RIP Jaybird. Love you brother.
A great mother.
Hehehe
Adorable
They were synchronized
Dawwwwww
MOOD

item.title: One of our most powerful enemy.
item.description: It's a book. A very highly recommended one. I briefly knew the author (sadly, he passed ten years ago), and he was one of the smartest persons I knew. This is not spam, the book is free to be read for personal use on Google Books here: https://books.google.it/books?id=jwfhUHuugrgC (you could also buy it, of course, but this version is complete).
item.dc:creator: PaperinoVB

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1314 (constantintotaled)
item.description:
Another day, another opportunity to get this bread.
Oops.
Pippin remembers he is a dinosaur.
Target market acquired.
Prehistoric Puffin escaping from stampeding mammoth.
The only part about this untrue is that there are surprisingly few snake-handling churches in Arizona: namely, zero that I'm aware of.
It's always nice to see a Tenontosaurus that's NOT a prop for an attacking Deinonychus pack
GNU Terry Pratchett
Big mood.
I love how this is a DOUBLE joke in the lore: that whole Noldor Kinslaying thing where they weren't given boats, and the mistreatment of Gimli's dad by Legolas' dad in The Hobbit.
10/10, would let her fill with my malice and greed or whatever.
I am absolutely going to use this if I ever get into another really bad accident that I survive nearly unscathed from.
I think I may add this to my list of stock responses that otherwise includes only "It is what it is," and "the horrors persist, but so do I."
Mood.
The worst sermon I ever heard is that we have no right to critique the government because we're not in medieval Byzantium anymore, and our words would fall on even more deaf ears than the Emperors of old. Like, uh, wtf?
10/10 would wear this shirt. (You are now hearing the soundtrack and new villager shhh-ahhhh sound and feeling nostalgia)
Hopefully, someone feels about me this way someday. But it's okay if it never happens.
You don't have to do all of the things to resist. Do what you can.
Oh thank God. I saw GF dances at first and worried that your girlfriend was dancing here to pay off her predatory student loans.
Mood.
We need more of these in the US right now.
Alexander hopped up on White Monster is a terrifying thought to me.
See also: Aragorn, son of Arathorn, Faramir, son of Denethor, arguably Sir Samuel Vimes, Duke of Ankh and Carrot Ironfoundersson.
The newly described earliest dinosaur from North America, Ahvaytum bahndooiveche, was named by the Shoshone people whose ancestral land this dinosaur was found on.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Glory! Whatdoin? Peekaboo!" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on.

item.title: I've lost 60 pounds in the last year. Last night I wrote a costume that I NEVER would have dared to put on before.
item.description:
215 pounds - obese, constantly in pain and short of breath, hiding my body in layers of loose-fitting clothes to try and disguise my shape. I was miserable and knew something had to change. I rarely allowed photos to be taken of myself because I hated looking at them. I actually don't have a picture of myself at my heaviest for this reason, but this selfie is close.
Flash forward to a year later! My husband insisted that he take this picture of me on our date last night. I objected as usual but he wasn't having it. "No. I want you to see yourself how I'm seeing you right now." Thanks, sweetheart. I had no idea how much I needed this.
item.dc:creator: torchit

item.title: The Cummunist
item.description: The Cummunist: Pleasure for all, not just the privileged few! This card vibrates with the energy of liberation, because no one should be left behind in the pursuit of satisfaction. Whether solo or in solidarity, the movement is strong and the rhythm is steady! Remember: true cumrades make sure everyone reaches the climax of the revolution! If anyone wants to see more of my embroidery work: https://rebordacaoembroidery.etsy.com

item.title: In 2019,a group of Japanese researchers set out to test an unusual theory rooted in decades of zoological speculation: that zebra stripes evolved as a defense mechanism against biting flies. The idea was based on earlier observations
item.description: In 2019, a group of Japanese researchers set out to test an unusual theory rooted in decades of zoological speculation: that zebra stripes evolved as a defense mechanism against biting flies. The idea was based on earlier observations suggesting that zebra patterns may confuse the visual systems of insects like horseflies and tsetse flies, making it harder for them to land. To put this to the test in a practical agricultural context, scientists from the Aichi Agricultural Research Center painted six Japanese black cows with either black-and-white zebra-like stripes, black stripes, or no stripes at all. Over nine days, they monitored how many biting flies landed on each cow and found that the zebra-striped cows experienced more than a 50% reduction in fly landings compared to the others. This experiment provided some of the strongest empirical support yet for the insect-deterrent function of zebra stripes. The implications of the study extend beyond quirky visual experiments. Biting flies don’t just cause discomfort—they also transmit diseases and reduce livestock productivity. Traditional solutions like pesticides can be harmful to the environment and are often costly for farmers. The idea that a simple, non-toxic, and low-cost method like painting stripes could protect animals introduces a potential game-changer for livestock management, especially in regions plagued by biting insects. This research also contributes to the broader scientific debate about why zebras have stripes in the first place—a mystery that has puzzled naturalists since Darwin’s time, with theories ranging from camouflage and social bonding to thermoregulation and predator confusion. But with this study, the insect-deterrence theory has gained some serious traction.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: MRW I find out that my friend is moving back home to Canada after living in Texas for a year and a half.
item.description: Maybe it was because of that guy that killed a cop outside her workplace then hurt a K9 and then he finally got gunned down at the back door of her workplace. That was a lot for a Canadian.
